mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an imminent labor market contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as earnings revisions turn downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100.0 thousand
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew when the trigger activates, or the signal's predictive power decays to zero within 6 months of out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and housing starts indicates a supply-demand mismatch that compresses corporate margins; when industrial output accelerates while housing construction stalls, equity returns decline over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO growth minus HOUST growth) fails to produce a positive Rank IC above 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 60-day returns show no significant difference between high-divergence and low-divergence regimes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active rate hikes, elevated core inflation readings force the market to price in prolonged liquidity withdrawal, which suppresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% annualized during active rate hikes, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below zero over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP per quarter reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as institutional flows chase growth.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth fails to correlate positively with 5-day forward SPX returns, or the rolling 12-month correlation coefficient remains below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate acts as a leading indicator for consumer spending contraction, which reduces equity risk premiums and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day equity returns do not decline when UNRATE crosses 6.5%, or the signal produces false breakouts in more than 40% of historical instances since 1990.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising short-term rates and cooling employment creates a policy dilemma that forces equity repricing; when FEDFUNDS increases while PAYEMS growth decelerates, market volatility spikes and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The cross-product of FEDFUNDS changes and PAYEMS growth momentum fails to explain more than 2% of the variance in 20-day forward returns, or the interaction signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a constrained supply environment, a sudden acceleration in industrial production signals capacity expansion that eases input cost pressures, boosting profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns do not exhibit positive skew when INDPRO accelerates above the threshold during supply bottlenecks, or the signal's predictive decay exceeds 30 days in out-of-sample validation.
